commit.message:
Fix doc comment XML stripping, escape tolerance, verbatim prefix, parser crashes (#547)

- ExtractDocComment: Strip inline XML tags (see, c, code, paramref, typeparamref)
  from doc comment content instead of preserving raw XML. Prevents unparseable tokens.
- Lexer: Tolerate unknown escape sequences (\S, \N, \D, etc.) by emitting the literal
  character instead of reporting Calor0004 errors. Both string scanner paths updated.
- CSharpToCalorConverter: Strip C# verbatim identifier prefix (@) from namespace names.
- RoslynSyntaxVisitor: Strip @ from namespace names in EnterNamespace and using directives.
- Parser (ParseValue): Accept @identifier in section marker attributes, stripping the @.
  Fixes existing .calr files with @is, @event etc. (11 Humanizer Icelandic files now compile).
- Parser (ParsePrimaryExpression): Replace throw with diagnostic + error recovery.
  Fixes 103+ parser crashes across Newtonsoft/Humanizer.
- Update escape sequence test, bump to v0.3.9.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix HelixTestRunner using the wrong version of dotnet-ef (#65676)

* Allow HelixTestRunner to restore prerelease versions of dotnet-ef

* Fixup

* Fixup again

* Pin Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design version in templates

The Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Tools package depends on
Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Design >= 8.0.0 (minimum). NuGet resolves
this transitive dependency to 8.0.0, which is incompatible with the
11.0.0-preview.3 Abstractions assembly (the AbstractionsStrings.ArgumentIsEmpty
method signature changed). This causes MissingMethodException when running
dotnet-ef migrations on template projects.

Fix by adding an explicit PackageReference for Design with the correct
version in the template csproj.in files, so the transitive dependency
from Tools is overridden with a version-matched package.

* fix(tests): resolve ModelLoader.load_model wrong-patch pattern across all serving tests

- Fix all 8 wrong ModelLoader.load_model patches in test_inference_chaos.py and
  test_inference_performance.py — InferenceServer never calls ModelLoader; correct
  injection point is ModelServer.predict
- Rewrite 3 TestCachePerformance tests to test actual server behaviour (pre-loaded
  stub model, no per-name LRU cache) instead of a ModelLoader abstraction that
  does not exist in the current architecture
- Remove unused `from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch` import from
  test_inference_performance.py now that no mocks are needed there
- Retire two xfail conftest.py entries (test_cache_eviction_performance,
  test_cache_vs_no_cache_performance) — tests are now green (13/13 passed,
  was 11 passed + 2 xfailed)
- All 16 chaos tests and 105 serving tests now pass

Root cause (pattern, not one-off): every test that patched
`src.codex_ml.serving.model_loader.ModelLoader.load_model` was a silent
no-op because create_app() pre-loads the model via InferenceServer.load_model()
and never delegates to ModelLoader.  Tests that asserted on mock.call_count or
mock.side_effect were therefore always testing nothing.

rivet invocation for read+write.

system temp directory. Refactor tests to use an autouse fixture that
redirects history to an isolated file and disables the guard.

* Add lattice-based vug sensitivity analysis notebook

- Implemented a new notebook for lattice-based vug sensitivity analysis.
- The notebook includes generation of stochastic baseline realizations, insertion of various vug shapes, and comparison of porosity and permeability.
- Added functions for network geometry updates, vug insertion, and result visualization.
- Included detailed markdown documentation outlining the methodology, scientific caveats, and results interpretation.

* Add lattice-based vug sensitivity analysis notebook

* Add simplified 2D lattice-based vug sensitivity analysis notebook

- Implemented a new notebook for studying the sensitivity of vugs in a 2D lattice network.
- Included functionality for generating stochastic baseline networks with pore/throat radius perturbations.
- Added methods for inserting vug super-pores and updating network geometry.
- Implemented analysis of porosity and permeability responses for various vug shapes.
- Integrated visualization capabilities using Plotly and Matplotlib for result presentation.
- Added detailed markdown documentation throughout the notebook for clarity and guidance.

* Add simplified 2D lattice-based vug sensitivity analysis notebook

- Renamed `PLOTLY_MAX_THROATS` to `PLOTLY_MAX_THROATS_BASELINE` in both 2D and 3D notebooks.
- Introduced `PLOTLY_MAX_THROATS_VUG` to manage throat visibility for vug connections.
- Modified the `save_network_png_matplotlib` and `save_network_png_matplotlib_2d` functions to handle optional throat limits.

string lookup failure. Unquoted patterns allow POSIX sh/bash to expand
the glob and pass the matched file paths to node.

Root cause: .env.development sets HTTPS_KEY_PATH=./certs/dev/server.key which

The old OR-based logic short-circuited on the env var value without checking

to HTTP. Playwright then received HTTP response on HTTPS connection → EPROTO.

Fixed issues:

- Falls back to default path (./certs/server.key) if env path does not exist

commit.message:
ci: split workflows and add E2E simulator run (#36)

Split monolithic build.yml into three focused workflows:
- build.yml: Build and publish NuGet packages
- test.yml: Parallel test matrix (AppleDev, AppleDev.Tool, AppleDev.FbIdb)
- run.yml: E2E simulator lifecycle (create, boot, install, launch, screenshot, logs, uninstall)

E2E workflow uses a pre-built MAUI test app that logs a startup marker,
validated via CLI tool commands following a Do/Validate pattern modeled
after the Android SDK Tools workflow.

Other changes:
- Rename AppleDev.Tool.Tests → AppleDev.Tool.Test for consistent *.Test.csproj naming
- Fix --output path validation (GetInvalidFileNameChars → GetInvalidPathChars)
- Guard cleanup steps against empty SIMULATOR_UDID
---------

commit.message:
fix: reliable activity detection and accurate timestamps for saved sessions

Two fixes:

1. Activity detection: widened from 60s to 5 minutes and now checks
   events.jsonl, workspace.yaml, AND checkpoints/index.md. Long tool
   calls (builds, tests) can go minutes without writing events.jsonl,
   but workspace.yaml and checkpoints update more frequently. This
   prevents the yellow dot from flickering on and off.

2. Timestamp display: saved sessions now show time since last file
   modification (last_modified) instead of the DB's updated_at which
   only flushes at turn boundaries. An active session shows '2m ago'
   instead of '6h ago'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 403: replace XorCsrfTokenRequestAttributeHandler with plain handler for SPA double-submit-cookie CSRF (#41)

* Initial plan

* fix: replace XorCsrfTokenRequestAttributeHandler with CsrfTokenRequestAttributeHandler to allow SPA double-submit-cookie CSRF pattern

Spring Security 6 defaults to XorCsrfTokenRequestAttributeHandler, which
tries to XOR-decode the X-XSRF-TOKEN header before comparing it to the
stored cookie. When a browser SPA sends the raw XSRF-TOKEN cookie value as
the header (Double Submit Cookie pattern), the XOR decode fails (27 decoded
bytes < 36 UUID token bytes → null → CSRF mismatch → 403 Forbidden).

Fix: configure CsrfTokenRequestAttributeHandler (plain handler) which
compares the submitted header value directly to the stored cookie value.

Also adds configurable security.csrf.cookie-domain and
security.csrf.cookie-same-site properties so operators can set
Domain=espacogeek.com and SameSite=Strict in production.

Adds post_SpaCsrfCookieFlow_ShouldReturn200 test that reproduces the exact
real-browser SPA flow: first POST → 403 + cookie; second POST with raw
cookie value as header → 200.

Co-authored-by: vitorhugo-java <65777252+vitorhugo-java@users.noreply.github.com>
